frameworks.name: large-scale cortical network switching
frameworks.one_line: blinks index transitions between dorsal-attention and default-mode networks

frameworks.name: attentional refresh
frameworks.one_line: blinks trigger a transient boost in visual attention deployment

core_question: Do spontaneous eye blinks produce measurable perceptual consequences that justify their high frequency?
key_assumptions: spontaneous blinks exceed what is required for corneal tear-film maintenance
blinks can index cortical network state changes
post-blink intervals can be precisely timed relative to stimulus onset
manipulations: interval between spontaneous blink and visual target onset
visual discrimination task at varying post-blink delays
key_findings: discrimination performance was selectively enhanced for ~200 ms following a spontaneous blink
interpretations: spontaneous blinks trigger an attentional refresh that transiently boosts visual processing
